instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Benefits of Having Multiple Term Insurance Policies
Term insurance plans can give you and your family financial security against uncertainty. Term plans are the basic essence of life insurance as they provide financial security against premature death. They protect a family from a possible strain by compensating them for the financial loss that they might suffer if the breadwinner passes away suddenly.
However, the true worth of a term insurance policy is realized when the sum assured is adequate. In the case of inadequate coverage, the family might be left wanting more and, despite the coverage, the financial responsibilities might not be met.
That is why you should always opt for an optimal sum assured when buying a term insurance policy. But what if you cannot afford the premium? Or, what if you already have a plan where the sum assured is not very high? How do you opt for a high sum assured?
One of the ways to do so is through multiple term insurance plans. Let us understand, but, first, the basics.
What is term insurance?
Term insurance is a type of life insurance policy that covers the risk of premature death. If the life insured dies during the policy tenure, the term plan pays a guaranteed benefit to the family.
Multiple term plans – the concept
Multiple term plans mean buying two or more term insurance policies. This gives you additional coverage and can help you avail of an optimal sum assured based on your financial needs.
Benefits of multiple term insurance plans
Multiple term insurance plans prove relevant in many instances. Here is the benefits of having more than one policy –
Possibility to avail of coverage for a longer period
Different term plans come with different tenures. By opting for different maturity tenures, you can increase the overall coverage duration. Moreover, if you have an existing term insurance plan with a limited policy tenure and then you buy another term plan with the whole life option, you get insured longer.
For instance, consider the following cases assuming you are 35 years old –
While Policy A covers you only till 65 years of age, with Policy B, you enjoy longer coverage duration. Moreover, if you also buy Policy C, you get covered lifelong.
Might prove affordable
Though a high sum assured is desirable, it might not be pocket-friendly, especially when you have just started your career and your income is limited. In such cases, rather than delaying the coverage, you can start small. You can buy a term insurance plan with a low sum assured which is affordable on your pockets. Later, as your income increases with age and experience, you can supplement the coverage by buying additional term plans. Thus, multiple-term plans allow you to afford higher coverage as you step up the coverage when you can afford it.
Minimizing the strain of claim rejections
In some term insurance plans, your claims might get rejected if you do not follow the correct claim procedure or due to any other reason. However, when you buy multiple policies, the financial effect of such rejections reduces. While one insurer might reject your claim, if the other insurers settle the claim, your family will not suffer a complete financial loss.
Possibility of enjoying additional riders
While all term plans allow optional riders, some plans come with inbuilt options that allow better coverage. You can choose such term plans even when you have existing plans to widen the scope of coverage with inbuilt riders.
Higher financial pay-outs
The primary benefit of having multiple term insurance policies is that the pay-out is higher. Every policy pays the death benefit if the life insured passes away during the policy tenure. The policy payouts from multiple plans might aggregate into a considerable amount and meet your family’s financial needs comfortably.
Get higher coverage with increasing responsibilities
When you are young, you might have limited responsibilities. As such, you might make do with a limited sum assured. However, as you get married, start a family, plan a child, and get more financial responsibilities, you need to step up your coverage. At such junctions of life, you can use the term plan calculator to know the increased coverage needed. Then, you can opt for multiple term insurance plans for supplementing the coverage to meet the increased responsibilities that you have.
Enhanced tax saving
Section 80C1 allows a tax deduction on the premium that you pay for term insurance plans with a limit of Rs.1.5 lakhs. As you opt for multiple term insurance policies, the aggregate premium that you pay for them goes up. As the premiums increase, you get to claim higher deductions. Higher deductions mean higher tax savings as your overall tax liability is reduced.
Multiple term insurance plans can be a suitable solution when –
You can’t afford a high sum assured under a single policy at once
Your financial responsibilities have increased since you last bought the plan
You are getting additional benefits in new term plans
Or for any other reason that suits you. There is no limit to the number of policies that you can own. Just use the term plan calculator and know the right sum assured. Buy one policy or multiple plans but secure adequate coverage. Moreover, when buying multiple plans, inform the insurer of the existing policies to comply with the principle of good faith. Buy multiple plans and enjoy the benefits that they have to offer. You can enjoy higher coverage without burning your pockets in the process.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
